title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Language Clinic: Deque Frontier
summary: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the deque frontier increment in deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-deque-frontier — Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_bfs_shortest_path_3(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Deque Frontier model · Theory Deque Frontier, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Deque Frontier precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Deque Frontier model · Theory Deque Frontier, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8119 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Deque Frontier model · Theory Deque Frontier, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Removing index zero from a list has constant cost.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-deque-frontier-1hjq4w.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-deque-frontier-1hjq4w(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_deque_frontier_1hjq4w\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reported_edges != oracle_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?predecessor_links != discovered - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fifo_order_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
